model.label: Gemini 3 Flash
model.description: Google DeepMind • Fast Gemini model optimized for low-latency reasoning, multimodal inputs, and tool use.

pick.rationale: Iowa enters this matchup with a superior offensive profile, ranking in the top 30 nationally in adjusted efficiency, which should allow them to exploit a Clemson defense missing its anchor. The Tigers suffered a significant blow with the season-ending ACL injury to starting center Carter Welling, leaving them vulnerable in the paint and on the glass. While Clemson's perimeter defense remains stout, Iowa's balanced attack and higher overall efficiency margin give them the edge in what is expected to be a tightly contested 8/9 battle.

reasoningStep.title: Iowa's Offensive Edge vs. Clemson's Interior Absence
reasoningStep.summary: Iowa's top-30 offense and superior overall efficiency metrics, combined with Clemson's loss of starting center Carter Welling, make the Hawkeyes the favorite in this matchup.
evidence: Iowa ranks 25th in KenPom and 26th in Torvik, while Clemson ranks 36th and 29th respectively.
Clemson starting center Carter Welling is out for the season with a torn ACL, impacting their 20th-ranked defense.
Iowa boasts a top-30 adjusted offensive efficiency (122.1), significantly higher than Clemson's 69th-ranked offense.

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.

Current game label: South 8/9
Round: Round of 64
Resolved teams: Clemson (clemson) vs Iowa (iowa)
Prior committed picks: 21
